module_name: Computer Science Concepts for Data Scientists
learning_objectives: Understand computer systems, operating systems, networks, virtualization and cloud computing; classify and describe application architectures; explain algorithms and key data structures; apply data processing based on filters and pipes; use visual variables and design principles for information visualization; use Generative AI for coding assistance.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Computer systems and networks; Topic 2: Algorithms and programming language concepts; Topic 3: Data processing and information visualization (big data 5 Vs, shell, Tableau)

learning_objectives: Apply the design of experiments to own quantitative research; select appropriate methods; conduct ANOVA, multi-factorial designs, randomization inference, power calculations and treatment randomization using R; use the specific language of experimental design.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Scientific methods and relationship between variables; Topic 2: Sampling; Topic 3: Threats to internal validity; Topic 4: Introduction to experiments; Topic 5: Analyzing experiments with two treatments; Topic 6: Analyzing experiments with more than two treatments; Topic 7: Implementation of an experiment

learning_objectives: Proficient in algebra and geometry of vectors and matrix algebra (inversion, determinants); set up and solve linear systems of equations; represent and process information using matrices; apply linear algebra methods using Python.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Vector geometry; Topic 2: Linear Equations (Gaussian elimination); Topic 3: Matrix algebra (inverse matrix, determinants)

learning_objectives: Work with structured and unstructured data; solve complex programming problems; develop algorithms; structure applications with OOP; deploy packages/libraries; evaluate the possibilities and limitations of Python.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Basics of Python (variables, data types, control structures); Topic 2: Advanced principles (functions, files, dictionaries, OOP); Topic 3: Modules (exception handling, iterators, generators, decorators)

learning_objectives: Test hypotheses; model and analyze data via regression models; build models in the Bayesian context; decide which test to use; apply and interpret the statistics software R.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Introduction to R / exploratory data analysis; Topic 2: Probability theory; Topic 3: Hypothesis testing; Topic 4: Regression models; Topic 5: Introduction to Bayesian thinking

learning_objectives: Design a real-world inspired data science process including collection, pre-processing, enrichment and presentation; develop and answer research questions; collect, prepare and inspect large data volumes; collaborate using git.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Data Acquisition (BeautifulSoup, Selenium, APIs, web-scraping); Topic 2: Cleansing and Pre-processing (Numpy, Pandas); Topic 3: Visualization, Presentation (Matplotlib, Plotly, Seaborn); Topic 4: Git, Web-frameworks (Streamlit, Dash, Flask, Django)

learning_objectives: Plan a database application; install and operate an SQL database server; define a database schema; load and transform data; perform SQL data analysis; optimize query runtime; visualize results in an interactive dashboard.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Database Management; Topic 2: Database Modeling (ER, relational, normal forms, DDL); Topic 3: Database Integration (DML, ETL/ELT, LOAD); Topic 4: Database Analysis (SELECT, joins, aggregation, subqueries); Topic 5: Database Optimization (execution plan, materialized views, indexes); Topic 6: Database Visualization (Metabase BI)

learning_objectives: Recognize the importance of data quality; select, apply and evaluate data quality software, tools and techniques; understand possibilities and limitations of data quality concepts; apply data quality language in reports.
learning_content_structure: 1. Data quality principles; 2. Basic properties; 3. Data quality dimensions; 4. Detecting/improving data quality; 5. ML methods / model-driven development; 6. Data quality in big data and social media

module_name: Applied Machine Learning and Predictive Modelling 1
learning_objectives: Know basic supervised learning procedures (regression, classification, SVM, resampling, model selection, regularization); identify and apply appropriate methods; interpret software results; interact critically with generative AI.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Linear Regression; Topic 2: Generalised Linear Models; Topic 3: Extensions (polynomials, GAMs); Topic 4: Support Vector Machines; Topic 5: Neural Networks; Topic 6: Approximate Bayesian / SVM; Topic 7: Model Validation

module_name: Applied Machine Learning and Predictive Modelling 2
learning_objectives: Know procedures of modern supervised and unsupervised learning (PCA, dimension reduction, MDS, clustering, trees, random forest, boosting); identify, apply and evaluate adequate methods on real data.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Classification (logistic regression); Topic 2: Trees and ensemble methods (random forest, boosting); Topic 3: Dimensionality reduction (PCA); Topic 4: Multi-dimensional Scaling (MDS, t-SNE); Topic 5: Clustering (K-means, PAM, hierarchical, DBSCAN)

module_name: Discrete Response, Time Series and Panel Data
learning_objectives: Understand and list methods for analysing time-dependent data; identify and quantify structures in time-dependent data; make well-founded predictions; compare methods for suitability; apply and interpret results.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Decomposition, noise treatment; Topic 2: Quantitative description (correlation functions); Topic 3: Modeling (AR, MA, ARMA, ARIMA, SARIMA, GARCH); Topic 4: Forecasting; Topic 5: Panel data (fixed effect models); Topic 6: Discrete response (probit/logit)

learning_objectives: Explain and compare deep learning methods for computer vision and computational language technologies; implement, train, debug and optimize neural networks; select suitable models and justify the choice; explain foundations/limitations of Generative AI.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Motivation and history (classical image classification, KNN, SVM, SGD); Topic 2: Core concepts (fully connected NN, CNN, RNN/LSTM/GRU); Topic 3: Advanced topics (Transformers, attention); Topic 4: Application (image/text classification, image captioning)

learning_objectives: Preliminary scientific study for the substantive and methodological preparation of the Master's Thesis. (Variants: W.MSCIDS_PMT03 without Project-based Learning, no module prerequisite; W.MSCIDS_PRS03 with Project-based Learning, requires W.MSCIDS_BYD12.)
learning_content_structure: Preliminary scientific study as an independent teaching and learning format preparing the main study

learning_objectives: Master's Thesis (no separate module description provided; dependency on Preliminary Study inferred from programme structure).
learning_content_structure: (not provided in module descriptions)

learning_objectives: Familiar with vector subspaces, bases, dimension, inner products, orthonormality; perform eigenvalue and singular value decomposition; visualize linear structures in high-dimensional spaces; apply via Python.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Euclidean vector space (subspace, span, basis, scalar products, norms, orthonormality); Topic 2: Linear maps; Topic 3: Matrix decompositions (eigenvalues, diagonalization, SVD)

learning_objectives: Manipulate, visualise and analyse data and report results to clients/stakeholders in static or interactive ways using R; interact critically with generative AI.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Reading data into R; Topic 2: Data preparation and manipulation; Topic 3: Graphical analyses (ggplot2); Topic 4: Dynamic reporting (Rmarkdown, Knitr, Quarto); Topic 5: R packages and beyond (APIs, maps); Topic 6: Shiny Apps; Topic 7: Other/optional (functions, parallel computing)

module_name: DevOps - Git, Docker & Software Engineering in Python
learning_objectives: Use Git/GitHub to collaborate; deploy code through CI/CD pipelines; work with Docker; collaborate in Scrum teams; break down complex requirements and build software as a team.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: DevOps Tools (Git, Docker, GitHub Actions, Jira); Topic 2: Software Development (OOP, unit testing, typing, debugging, documentation); Topic 3: DevOps Practices (Scrum, CI/CD, code review, microservices/FastAPI); Topic 4: Exercises (multiplayer games)

learning_objectives: Design and implement generative AI applications using no-code frameworks and native Python libraries; critically analyze and optimize generative AI systems; apply ethical considerations.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Motivation and history; Topic 2: Building blocks (neural networks, GANs, VAEs, autoregressive models); Topic 3: GenAI in practice (no-coding tools, DALL-E, GPT); Topic 4: Python for Generative AI; Topic 5: Advanced topics (fine-tuning, creative applications); Topic 6: Ethical considerations and future directions

learning_objectives: Understand the design process and use its tools; identify and evaluate stakeholder requirements; define projects from a user perspective; apply human-centered research and design methods; plan, conduct and evaluate workshops; use AI consciously in the design process.
learning_content_structure: Understand relevance of human-centered design for data science; mindset, concepts, processes and methods of human-centered design; tools of the design process, selecting and applying methods to project phases

learning_objectives: Fundamentals of data visualization and design; select visualization based on the goal of analysis; build dashboards in Tableau; articulate a problem statement and the 'so what'; create a ghost deck.
learning_content_structure: Session 1: Intro to Data Visualization and Design Principles; Session 2: Creating visualizations in Tableau; Session 3: Build Data Visualization / planning phase; Session 4: Design phase and dashboards in production; Session 5: Industry dashboard designs and mini-projects

module_name: Scientific Writing and Presentation Skills - Input and Coaching
learning_objectives: Understand basic forms of scientific writing and APA citation; plan, structure, write and edit scientific texts; analyze and meet audience expectations; design and deliver convincing professional presentations; apply Anglo-American conventions. (Requires concurrent enrolment in the Preliminary Master's Thesis.)
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Style; Topic 2: Planning; Topic 3: Success factors; Topic 4: Spoken communication; Topic 5: Anglo-American conventions

learning_objectives: Familiar with data ethics, algorithm/AI ethics and Corporate Digital Responsibility (CDR); know criteria for assessing ethical conflicts; evaluate and improve design variants per ethical criteria; conduct ethical stakeholder analysis. (Module description provided under code W.MSCIDS_EBD03 'Ethical Issues of Big Data', coordinator Gordon Millar.)
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Digital society and ethics; Topic 2: Data ethics; Topic 3: Algorithm/AI Ethics (value-based design, EU AI Act); Topic 4: Corporate Digital Responsibility (CDR)

learning_objectives: Define and execute a data-driven strategy with IT governance objectives; understand impact of missing compliance and bad data governance; build a policy strategy hands-on in the Azure cloud; obtain Microsoft SC-900 certification. (Further: at least one of Management of Digital Enterprise, Data-Driven Business Models, DWL03, ETL03 or BDL03_1.)
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Motivation and history (data strategy); Topic 2: Core concepts (data mapping, cloud governance, requirements engineering); Topic 3: Advanced topics (governance and policy in cloud); Topic 4: Application (Azure)

learning_objectives: Know legal aspects affected by data systems; understand data protection principles in Switzerland and EU; know Big Data-specific data protection concepts; know regulatory approaches for GenAI; evaluate design variants under data protection and fundamental rights aspects.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: General Data Protection requirements; Topic 2: Data Protection Rights of data subjects; Topic 3: Big Data-specific Data Protection aspects; Topic 4: Fundamental Rights aspects of Big Data; Topic 5: Legal Aspects of GenAI

module_name: Global School of Empirical Research Methods
learning_objectives: Enhance quantitative and qualitative statistics skills in block seminars taught by external faculty (University of St. Gallen GSERM programme).
learning_content_structure: Varies by chosen GSERM course (see gserm.ch/stgallen/courses)

learning_objectives: Use generative AI to solve real-world business problems; conduct requirements elicitation, design and develop prototype solutions using generative AI cloud services; master prompt engineering; build robust data models.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Motivation and Project (enterprise AI design thinking, conversational design, WatsonX); Topic 2: Core concepts (generative language models, IBM Cloud, Watson AI services, prompt engineering, hyperparameter tuning, chatbots); Topic 3: Advanced topics (knowledge databases, pipelines, APIs, web services, user interfaces)

learning_objectives: Use a multi-sensor system to measure and visualize social and individual energy patterns in distributed teams; apply Human Dynamics, Social Network Analysis and machine learning; work in international Collaborative Innovation Networks (COINs).
learning_content_structure: Part 1: Introductory course on Human Dynamics / Social Network Analysis / machine learning (Happimetrics); Part 2: Large-scale project work in international teams with biweekly status updates and final paper

module_name: SAS Joint Certificate "SAS Business Analytics Expert"
learning_objectives: Joint HSLU/SAS certification covering SAS Viya, Visual Analytics, model building, SAS programming and machine learning; turn data into value across the analytics lifecycle.
learning_content_structure: Pathway 1: Visual Analytics and Modeling (Visual Business Analyst, Visual Modeling Specialist); Pathway 2: Machine Learning and SAS Programming (SAS Base Programming Specialist, Machine Learning Specialist)

learning_objectives: Conduct big-data analyses for managing customer experiences and relationships; carry out quantitative analyses on real market data; evaluate whether a question can be addressed with given data; use Generative AI for ideation and code generation; visualize and explain results.
learning_content_structure: Principles; Overview (CRM, customer, transaction, touchpoint, social media data); Analysis framework; Use cases (customer experience management, CRM, churn, cross-selling); Specific uses (case work); Presentation

module_name: Data Analytics for Energy Systems and IoT
learning_objectives: Understand terms in energy systems and IoT; understand methods for modeling target values from metadata/panel data, anomaly detection and time-dependent signals; compare and identify appropriate methods; apply to new data.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Principles of energy systems in Switzerland; Topic 2: Collecting energy system data; Topic 3: Remote diagnosis / smart maintenance (anomaly detection); Topic 4: Estimating residential energy demand (linear regression on panel data); Topic 5: Load forecasting (AR, MA, ARMA, ARIMA, neural networks); Topic 6: Internet of Things

learning_objectives: Understand fraud detection methodologies including information security and risk management; gain practical experience analyzing fraud data and setting up strategies, systems and procedures; basic knowledge of forensics and incident response.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Introduction (definitions, security, vulnerabilities, threats, risks); Topic 2: Strategies (fraud detection techniques, AI, ML); Topic 3: Tools (security operations, incident response, IT forensics); Topic 4: Solutions; Topic 5: Lab Exercise; Topic 6: Guest Lecture; Topic 7: Presentations

module_name: Geospatial Data Analysis for Smart Communities
learning_objectives: Prepare simple spatial data and convert it into thematic maps; interpret data in the context of smart communities; link types of georeferencing data (Swiss national coordinates, WGS-84); use visualization as communication; use QGIS and R.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Principles of the topic (smart communities); Topic 2: Principles of georeferencing; Topic 3: Simulations (FaLC, Senozon, 3D model); Topic 4: Movement patterns (mobile phone signals); Topic 5: Spatial data for political purposes; Topic 6: Data preparation (QGIS, R)

learning_objectives: Understand major topics in data science in health care; be familiar with different health-care data sources; prepare a data set for analysis; select and apply analytical methods; break down an analytical question into manageable parts; use GenAI to improve work.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Data Science in Health Care; Topic 2: Working with Data (sources, missing data, cleansing); Topic 3: Generative AI (GenAI); Topic 4: Presentation

learning_objectives: Understand, interpret and evaluate natural experiments; understand differences between experiments and natural experiments; estimate empirical models on natural experiments in R; perform robustness tests; critically assess natural experiments.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Experiments and Natural Experiments; Topic 2: Matching; Topic 3: Instrumental Variables; Topic 4: Difference-in-Differences; Topic 5: Regression Discontinuity Design

learning_objectives: Solid working knowledge of Tableau Prep and Tableau Desktop; evaluate possibilities and limitations of open data; visualize open and geospatial data; create interactive dashboards and maps from OpenStreetMap.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Introduction to the topic (open data, data commons); Topic 2: Data preparation (Tableau Prep, joins, aggregation); Topic 3: Visualizing data (Tableau, calculated fields, OpenStreetMap); Topic 4: Insights from practice (guest lecture)

learning_objectives: Understand measurement technologies and data collection methods in sport; select and apply analytical methods to performance questions; visualize information with D3; build performance indicators; present work convincingly.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Phenomena Sport; Topic 2: Methodological Approaches; Topic 3: Areas of Applications (analytics, profiling/scouting, athlete development, content generation); Topic 4: Sports Data Analytics as a Business

learning_objectives: Understand characteristics of financial market time series; apply appropriate methods pragmatically; know basic time series models and when to apply them; explain economic, methodological and applied aspects; discuss opportunities, risks and limitations.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Principles of financial markets; Topic 2: Applied fields (risk/portfolio management, forecasting, crypto, HFT, behavioral finance, GenAI); Topic 3: Applied methods (data preparation, estimation, ARIMA, GARCH, VAR); Topic 4: Application

module_name: Data-Driven Supply Chain Management and Logistics
learning_objectives: Carry out quantitative analyses of given data to improve supply network design and processes; identify input parameters and optimize parameterization; use case-specific SCM and logistics methods; visualize key insights and derive implications.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Motivation and history; Topic 2: Core concepts and methods (SCM, supply network resilience, network modelling, smart logistics); Topic 3: Advanced topics (data analysis methods); Topic 4: Application (Kühne+Nagel, Bossard, telecom use cases)

learning_objectives: Employ analytics to help solve sustainability-related problems including climate change; connect relevant data and domains; use a tool kit from data integration via time-series and lifecycle analysis to integrated assessment modeling and dynamic simulation; engage stakeholders.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Sustainability (SDGs, climate, ESG reporting); Topic 2: Challenge (real-life climate challenge in teams); Topic 3: Analytics methods (data sourcing, regression, time-series, system dynamics, agent-based and integrated assessment models); Topic 4: Stakeholder engagement

learning_objectives: Practice-integrated, project-based learning in interdisciplinary teams on real-world challenges from business or society; apply scientific methods, project management and innovation approaches; develop subject-specific, methodological and transferable competencies. (Module description: W.MSCIDS_BYD12, 12 ECTS.)
learning_content_structure: Real-world, practice-oriented challenges provided by companies or public institutions; structured learning boxes with academic and methodological coaching

learning_objectives: Accreditation of relevant professional experience (no separate module description provided).
learning_content_structure: (not provided in module descriptions)

learning_objectives: Explain and complete CRUD tasks for SQL and NoSQL databases; analyze lab tasks and decide which use cases suit relational vs NoSQL; query MongoDB using Python (PyMongo); build aggregation pipelines with Generative AI.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Python Database Programming (SQLite3, MySQL, PostgreSQL); Topic 2: NoSQL (SQL vs NoSQL, big data); Topic 3: MongoDB (database design, Atlas, PyMongo, aggregation pipelines)

learning_objectives: Configure, leverage and interact with a data lake; become familiar with the Azure big data infrastructure; interact with a data lake using Python and present on a dashboard; determine adequate big data architecture and write a full offering with pricing, lifecycle and SLA.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Big Data in Business; Topic 2: Data Handling (cloud databases, data lakes); Topic 3: Cloud Coverage (Azure, Data Factory, Databricks); Topic 4: Data handling (Python, Power BI/Tableau, deployment concept)

learning_objectives: Build the main components of an NLP pipeline; translate business use cases into NLP problems; founded knowledge in pre-processing, full-text search, bag-of-words, document classification, word embeddings, language models, NER, summarization; deep learning in NLP.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: NLP Pipeline & Linguistic Concepts; Topic 2: Text Processing & Exploratory Text Analysis; Topic 3: Representation learning (word embeddings); Topic 4: Similarity & Classification, Intro to Deep Learning (NN+CNN); Topic 5: RNN + attention; Topic 6: Transformers and LLMs; Topic 7: LLMs and advanced concepts

learning_objectives: Explain characteristics of image data; implement, train, debug and optimize neural networks for image classification and detection; explain and compare deep learning methods for image-based object classification; explain foundations and limitations of Generative AI.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Motivation, history and image fundamentals (NumPy/OpenCV); Topic 2: Core vision tasks and evaluation (segmentation, metrics); Topic 3: Modern architectures (CNN, Vision Transformers, detectors, segmentation); Topic 4: Generative and multimodal vision (VAEs, GANs, diffusion, CLIP); Topic 5: Application and project work

learning_objectives: Integrate LLMs into the complete software development lifecycle; use LLM-powered coding assistants and prompt engineering; build development agents and implement LLMOps; master both LLMs for DevOps and operating AI systems (LLMOps).
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: LLMs as Coding Assistants; Topic 2: LLMs as App and Experience Builders; Topic 3: LLMs for DevOps; Topic 4: LLMOps - DevOps for LLMs; Topic 5: AgentOps - Code as Agents; Topic 6: RAG for Coding - Building Organizational Copilots

module_name: Data Warehouse and Data Lake Systems
learning_objectives: Explain data warehouse and data lake concepts; data and schema integration in DW/data lake systems; data analysis and visualization using DW/data lake systems; analyze and resolve practical problems; work with AWS services.
learning_content_structure: Data lake intro (evolution, schema on read); Data ingestion (ETL, Lambda, REST APIs); Data storage (Apache Airflow, AWS S3, RDS); AWS modern data architecture; Data warehouse systems; Data visualization (QuickSight)

learning_objectives: Practical knowledge of modern data engineering (cloud, data lake, big data infrastructure); build data pipelines independently; implement data quality in pipelines; implement a real-time data process; bridge the gap between business and IT/DWH specialists.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Introduction and Motivation; Topic 2: Foundations and storage technologies (NoSQL, data lakes, cloud); Topic 3: Introduction to ELT (pipelines, Apache HOP); Topic 4: Real-time streaming and data science tools (Apache Kafka); Topic 5: ELT in production; Topic 6: Data scraping and Robotic Process Automation

learning_objectives: Understand technical foundations of LLMs and their applications; select, fine-tune and deploy LLMs for specific tasks; advanced prompt engineering and multimodal AI interactions; manage costs and resources of LLM deployment.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Introduction & building blocks (transformers, attention, tokenization); Topic 2: The LLM you need (zero-shot, few-shot, RAG, fine-tuning); Topic 3: Interacting (prompting, multimodal); Topic 4: Training LLMs (pretraining, fine-tuning, RLHF, DPO); Topic 5: LLMs on a budget (LoRA, PEFT); Topic 6: Practical course; Topic 7: Multimodal LLM (optional)

learning_objectives: Articulate theoretical foundations of Graph Neural Networks and causal inference with graphical models; apply suitable GNN architectures and causal models to tasks such as node classification, link prediction and graph classification; reason about causal structures.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Motivation and history; Topic 2: Core concepts and methods; Topic 3: Advanced topics; Topic 4: Application (guided / assignment / exercise)

learning_objectives: Explain and contrast main machine learning methods for processing audio data; explain attributes of audio signals; familiar with Bayesian decision, clustering, statistical learning, neural networks, kernel methods and Markovian models; apply to audio analysis, synthesis and transformation.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Acoustics; Topic 2: Signals (time/frequency domain, Fourier, spectrograms); Topic 3: Applications (synthesis, transformation, analysis); Topic 4: Models I (sequential data, Hidden Markov Model, Viterbi); Topic 5: Models II (NN, RNN, attention & transformers); Topic 6: Intelligent Virtual Assistants

learning_objectives: Understand fundamentals of recommendation systems (Bayesian methods, user/item-based collaborative filtering, matrix factorization, knowledge-graph recommendations); implement, train, tune and evaluate a recommendation system in Python.
learning_content_structure: Topic 1: Fundamentals of recommender systems; Topic 2: Content-based recommender systems; Topic 3: Collaborative filtering; Topic 4: Matrix Factorization; Topic 5: Other model-based collaborative filtering; Topic 6: Knowledge graph-based recommender systems; Topic 7: Generative AI in recommender systems; Topic 8: Real-world challenges

learning_objectives: Transform a product-oriented business model into a digital, data-driven business model; understand enabling technologies and disruptive forces; develop digital strategies, maturity models and use cases. (Module description present but module not listed in the 2026 course catalogue.)
learning_content_structure: Day 1: Megatrend Digitalisation and enabling technologies; Day 2: Individualization, socialisation, automation, disruptive forces; Day 3: Business model innovation, digital business models and strategies; Day 4: Digital strategy, employment impact, AI ethics, cyber security; Day 5: Study trip and presentations
